skill.name: zaker-hot-news
skill.description: Fetches recent ZAKER hot news headlines from a public API and formats titles, summaries, authors, and publish times.
skill.summary: Get a quick digest of current ZAKER hot news headlines.
skill.author: ZAKER-coder

security_audit.summary: Static network and shell findings are mostly expected for a news-fetching skill that calls a public ZAKER endpoint. The high weak-crypto and entropy alerts are false positives in SKILL.md, and no prompt injection or malicious exfiltration evidence was found.

low_findings.title: Expected Network Access to Public News API
low_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE: The Python script and documentation make GET requests to a hardcoded ZAKER hot-news endpoint. This is the core function of the skill, and no credentials, environment variables, or local files are sent, but users should know it depends on a third-party service and untrusted remote content.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The static network findings match explicit GET requests to a public news endpoint. The surrounding code shows no secret access or user data exfiltration, so the residual risk is limited to external dependency and remote content trust.

low_findings.title: Shell Wrapper Runs curl and jq
low_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE: The shell script invokes curl and pipes the response to jq. The URL is fixed and quoted, so command injection is not evident, but the skill still executes external commands and processes remote API output.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The shell execution is directly visible and uses common tools with a hardcoded endpoint. I found no variable expansion from user input and no dynamic command construction.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backticks Misclassified as Command Execution
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: Many external-command alerts in SKILL.md are inline Markdown code spans that describe tool names, endpoint fields, or response fields. They are documentation, not executable code paths.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are Markdown documentation and response schema descriptions. They do not invoke a shell or interpolate user-controlled input.

low_findings.title: Weak Crypto and Entropy Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The static weak-cryptography alerts point to skill metadata and descriptive text, not cryptographic functions. The entropy alert points at the opening Markdown front matter, with no binary blob, encoded payload, or hidden script visible.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines contain YAML front matter and prose about news sources. I found no MD5, SHA1, cipher use, binary payload, or obfuscated content in those locations.

dangerous_patterns.title: Hardcoded Third-Party Endpoint
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill relies on a fixed external ZAKER API endpoint for all news retrieval. This is intentional, but it means output depends on a third-party service outside the marketplace.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The endpoint is plainly hardcoded and used by both scripts. The pattern is not malicious by itself, but it is a real operational and privacy consideration.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Command Use in Shell Script
dangerous_patterns.description: The shell helper executes curl and jq to fetch and format remote data. The command does not include user-supplied arguments, reducing injection risk.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command line is directly visible and fixed. There is no evidence of dynamic command construction, but it still requires external tooling.

content.value_statement: Users often need a fast overview of current news without opening many sites. This skill fetches ZAKER hot headlines and presents concise article summaries with sources and times.
actual_capabilities: Fetches the latest ZAKER hot-news article list from a public API.
Displays article titles, authors, publish times, and summaries.
Supports quick headline requests in Chinese and English.
Provides Python and shell examples for retrieving the feed.
Formats headline lists for fast scanning.
limitations: Requires network access to the ZAKER API endpoint.
Does not verify the factual accuracy of each news article.
Returns the feed provided by ZAKER, not every global news source.
The shell helper requires curl and jq for formatted output.

use_cases.title: Morning News Brief
use_cases.description: Get a short list of important headlines before starting work.

use_cases.title: Current Events Scan
use_cases.description: Review recent domestic and international topics from a single hot-news feed.

use_cases.title: Bilingual Headline Query
use_cases.description: Ask for news using Chinese or English phrases and receive a compact digest.

prompt_templates.title: Get Today Headlines
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a quick feed.
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me the latest ZAKER hot news headlines with short summaries.

prompt_templates.title: Summarize Major Events
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request for prioritizing important items.
prompt_templates.prompt: Fetch the latest hot news and group the most important stories by topic.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Briefing
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request for a structured digest.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the ZAKER hot news feed to create a concise briefing with source names and publish times.

prompt_templates.title: Compare News Themes
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for synthesis across the feed.
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze the current ZAKER hot-news list and identify recurring themes, major risks, and items that need follow-up verification.

output_examples.input: What is the latest news today?
output_examples.output: A numbered headline digest with each article title, author, publish time, and one summary line.

output_examples.input: Give me the biggest current events from ZAKER.
output_examples.output: A grouped briefing that separates national news, international news, and other trending topics.
一份简短热点新闻列表，包含标题、来源、发布时间和摘要。

best_practices: Ask for a brief digest when you need speed.
Request source names and publish times for context.
Verify important claims with primary sources before acting.
anti_patterns: Do not treat the feed as complete coverage of all news.
Do not use summaries as legal, medical, or financial advice.
Do not assume every remote article is verified by the skill.

faq.question: What news source does this skill use?
faq.answer: It uses a public ZAKER hot-news API endpoint documented in the skill files.

faq.question: Does it need an API key?
faq.answer: The skill documentation says the endpoint uses GET requests without authentication.

faq.question: Can it fetch breaking news?
faq.answer: It can fetch the current hot-news feed when the ZAKER endpoint is available.

faq.question: Does it work in Chinese and English?
faq.answer: The skill description and examples support news requests in both Chinese and English.

faq.question: Does it fact-check articles?
faq.answer: No. It retrieves and formats article data, but users should verify important claims.

faq.question: Why is there a security warning?
faq.answer: The skill uses network requests and a shell helper, so the marketplace should disclose those behaviors.
